title: DNA EXTRACTION USING PHENOL-CHLOROFORM

steps: Add 500 µl Laird’s buffer in 1,5 ml eppendorf tube, one for each sample.
Cut 10-30 µg of muscle tissue and put it in the tube with Laird’s buffer.
Add 20 µl Proteinase K (20 mg/ml) to each tube
Incubate overnight in movement at 56ºC (or at least 4 hours). * If your samples are not completely solved, add more Proteinase K and incubate for longer time.DAY 2:
Add 500 µl Phenol to each tube and shake heavily during 10 min.
Centrifuge for 10 min (4ºC) at 13000 rpm.
Pipette and keep all of the supernatant (top layer) into a new 1,5 eppendorf tube.
Add 500 µl Phenol-Chloroform-Isoamyl and shake heavily during 10 min.
Centrifuge for 10 min (4ºC) at 13000 rpm.
Pipette and keep all of the supernatant (top layer) into a new 1,5 eppendorf tube.
Add 500 µl Chloroform-Isoamyl and shake heavily during 10 min.
Centrifuge for 10 min (4ºC) at 13000 rpm.
Pipette and keep all of the supernatant (top layer) into a new 1,5 eppendorf tube. Be careful not to get anything of the down layer.
Add 0,1 volumes of 3M NaAC to each tube and mix it softly (do not use vortex). E.g.: 40 µl to 400 µl of supernatant.
Add 2 volumes of ice cold 95-100% ethanol (previously stored at -20ºC) to each tube E.g.: 800 µl to 400 µl of supernatant. Mix it softly (turn the tubes upside down).
Leave at -20ºC overnight (or at least 5-6 hours).DAY3:
Centrifuge for 30 min (4ºC) at 13000 rpm.
Pour off ethanol.
Add 1000 µl ice cold ethanol 70%.
Centrifuge for 15 min (4ºC) at 13000 rpm.
Pour off ethanol.
Centrifuge for 5 min (4ºC) at 13000 rpm.
Pour off residual ethanol.
Dry pellet completely by leaving the tube open at room temperature.
Dissolve pellet in 100 µl ddH2O.
creator: Santiago Montero-Mendieta
